log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable packed with BobSoft Mini Delphi (AsPack). It has runtime modules and an overlay. Now I need to get all IOC types to understand the scope of the investigation (IPs, doma

The IOC summary shows no network activity (no IPs, domains, or HTTP), but there are 2 registry persistence keys and 13 dropped files. The process activity showed 0 processes created, which seems incon

The dropped files reveal a clear pattern of social engineering and persistence. The malware drops files with double extensions (.exe appended to .exe, .html.exe, .jpg.exe) to bypass user suspicion. It

The registry changes confirm a Winlogon Shell persistence technique. The malware adds 'HelpMe.exe' to the Winlogon Shell, causing it to execute automatically at user logon alongside Explorer.exe. This
The process activity shows no processes created (which seems inconsistent with the command executions in the overview), but the notable modules include many system DLLs and some suspicious files like
